caption: 30° feels like 20°. Funny how we trust a weather app when it says a number doesn’t tell the whole story, but we let age define what we’re capable of. I’m 30. And instead of slowing down, I’m starting over. A new career. New goals. New dreams. The strongest, healthiest, most confident version of myself I’ve ever been. Age is just data. It doesn’t measure your courage, your ambition, your resilience, or your ability to reinvent yourself. You don’t expire at a certain number. You evolve. So here’s to starting late, starting again, and starting scared. Because life isn’t about acting your age—it’s about living on your own terms. 30 never felt this powerful. ✨ #ageisjustanumber #turning30 #careerrestart #glowup

“Perfect body” - What if I told you that what you see online is just a snapshot of a moment—not the whole story? We all get caught up in the illusion of the ‘perfect body’ we see on Instagram—the angles, the poses, the highlight reel. But here’s the truth: I do have stomach fat, lower body fat, and I’m working on it—every day. My Instagram might not always show that reality, but this journey is mine. Your day one doesn’t have to look like anyone else’s. Let’s stop hollow comparisons and remember: we’re all on different paths, and that’s okay. . #reelvsreality #ɢʏᴍɢɪʀʟ #instagramlife #reallife #stomachfat

Growing up, I was always compared, my skin tone was a constant topic, and that left me drowning in self-doubt. But I worked hard, and I fought for my confidence. Now, I stand on the other side. I don’t compare myself to others. I measure myself against who I was yesterday. I am my own competition. So, stop comparing yourself to others. Be your own inspiration, reflect on how far you’ve come, and keep pushing to be better than you were yesterday. And now, I don’t compete with anyone but myself. From self-doubt to self-love, I grew up. #SelfLoveJourney #OwnYourGlowUp #StopComparingStartLiving #FromSelfDoubtToSelfLove #beyourowninspiration

Maybe it’s the hopeless romantic in me, but I miss the era of films like Kismat connection —when flirting wasn’t hidden in cheesy pick-up lines. It lived in stolen glances, lingering eye contact, and the unspoken conversations that happened between two people across a room. There’s something incredibly intimate about the way someone looks at you. A single glance can carry curiosity, admiration, mischief, desire, or a thousand unsaid words. Eyes have a way of telling stories that lips often can’t. #kismatconnection #EyesDontLie #ModernRomance #UnspokenConnection #romanticthoughts

Life’s too short to keep explaining yourself to people who’ve already decided not to understand you.  No matter how much you give, someone will always find a reason to be disappointed. So choose yourself, protect your peace, and stop shrinking to fit other people’s expectations. At least if someone’s happy, let it be you. ✨ #ChooseYourself #MainCharacterEnergy #SelfLoveClub #HealingJourney #ConfidenceBoost

Day 3 of the 45-day challenge. Today, I focused on hydration and taking a mental health break after a tough breakup. My day started spiraling, so I shifted my rest day, stayed home, and allowed myself to just be. Even though I wasn’t in the best headspace, I still cooked my meals, stayed on track with nutrition, and didn’t give in to comfort food. I’m being a bit gentler with myself today, but I won’t give up. I’ll come back stronger tomorrow. Please keep supporting me, your well wishes mean the world.#nobingeeating #progressnotperfectio #45daychallenge #mentalhealthmatters stayconsistent

The reality is a lot less glamorous—measuring butter, hitting protein goals, dragging myself through workouts, and staying consistent even when I don’t feel like it. Day 2\/45 ✅ No shortcuts. No magic fixes. Just showing up every day and trusting the process. #45DayChallenge #AbsJourney #FitnessJourney #CalorieDeficit #ProteinGoals LegDay ConsistencyOverPerfection

Instagram vs Reality ✨ This is where I started. This is what I’m aiming for. And this is where I am right now. Social media can make everything look perfect — the angles, the poses, the lighting. But reality? I still have lower belly fat, bloating days, insecurities, and moments where I don’t feel my best. And that’s okay. 45 days I am going to stop chasing “perfect” and start chasing consistency instead. This journey is not about becoming unrecognizable. It’s about becoming stronger, healthier, and more confident in my own skin. No filters. No unrealistic expectations. Just progress.  #45DayChallenge #AbsJourney #InstagramVsReality #FitnessJourney #RealBody

Imagine waking up tomorrow and feeling completely at home in yourself; no masks, no apologies, just the version of you, you love most. This is the power of choosing yourself, even if that version doesn’t fit neatly into society’s boxes. You don’t have to shrink, hide, or pretend. You thrive when you embrace who you are, flaws and all. The world might not always understand you, but that doesn’t matter. What matters is the story you choose to live. A story of courage, growth, and unapologetic self-love. So, take that step, keep growing, and remember: your life is yours to shape, one fearless choice at a time.

I was hesitant to share this, and I still am. It feels like those “what’s on your mind?” kinda posts from Facebook back in the day (IFYKYK). It’s rare for my Instagram to have something so real, sad, and which is not just another reel. This isn’t a breakup story, just a girl trying to find true love in this f*cked up dating scenario, especially in a place like Delhi. Sometimes, I wonder if it’s bravery or just sheer foolishness to put yourself out there, to let someone else hold that much power over you, hoping they won’t crush it, and to be vulnerable, only to end up going nowhere. I feel exhausted and am losing hope to find the kind of love I desire (and deserve.) The best part is that—I know, no matter how dark it feels right now, I will be okay and reach a neutral place because I am the BIG girl from a small town, living in a big city, and who knows how to take care of herself. However, sadly, I don’t always wish to be that strong independent woman who faces life head-on, guides herself out of emotional turmoil, heals, and then finds the courage to do it all over again. But, I NEED to because I refuse to settle for the bare minimum. So, the bigger question is, will this be worth it? Absolutely! If this brings me the kind of care and love that feels safe, valued, respected, desired, and fosters growth and satisfaction. But, the mind plays games, and the question arises yet again, “What if it doesn’t?” And I ask myself, “Are you that person, who gives up so easily?” The answer is a resounding, NO. So, I gather myself, piece by piece, day by day. Even if today feels impossible, I may take as small a step as going to the gym, lifting some weights, and letting my heart out while stretching my sore body after an intense workout session.
Building, Rebuilding and growing. You always stand back up.
